question: [File: 'File Name: src/periphery/TakeAmountsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the market settlement fee decreases between the off-chain view call and the on-chain take execution trigger TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits(view) -> MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral -> Midnight.take (loop continues to next offer), violating the invariant that each offer is filled at most once per bundler call, causing scoped impact: unfair debt reduction where the buyer's debt is reduced by fewer units than the buyer assets paid? Proof idea: fuzz settlementFee decrease, assert creditOf(buyer) after take < expected from targetBuyerAssets.

[File: 'File Name: src/periphery/TakeAmountsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: sellerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged borrower-seller under the precondition that the market settlement fee increases between the off-chain view call and the on-chain take execution trigger TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits(view) -> MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget -> Midnight.take, violating the invariant that filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets, causing scoped impact: position desynchronization where the seller incurs more debt than the target seller assets imply? Proof idea: fuzz settlementFee increase, assert debtOf(seller) > expected from targetSellerAssets.
